text: Increased number of nasal lesions, refers to increased injury at the organ level.  Among the nasal lesions that are frequently observed are hyperplasia, metaplasia, degeneration, and inflammation (Hardisty et al. 1999; Harkema et al. 2006).  Preference is given to document location and tissue layer of the injury (Hardisty et al. 1999; Harkema et al. 2006).   The nose detects smells, filters out particles, and helps regulate air temperature and humidity.  Damage to the nose can impair ability to avoid odors or detect food.
